industries.name: Automotive & Transportation Lightweighting
uses: Die-cast magnesium alloys (AZ91D, AM60B) for steering wheels, instrument panel structures, seat frames, door inners, and transmission cases — 33% lighter than aluminum, 75% lighter than steel
Magnesium-aluminum alloys for engine blocks, oil pans, and cylinder head covers in high-performance and fuel-economy vehicles
Magnesium sheet alloys in development for automotive body panels — requires improved room-temperature formability versus aluminum
Bicycle frames and components, motorcycle engine casings, and aerospace seat structures where weight savings justify cost premium
Electric vehicle battery housings and structural components benefit from magnesium's combination of light weight and electromagnetic shielding properties

companies.name: Meridian Lightweight Technologies
companies.use: World's largest magnesium die-caster. Produces structural magnesium components for Ford, GM, FCA, and BMW including instrument panel beams, liftgate inners, and front-end carriers.

companies.name: Dynacast
companies.use: Global precision die-casting company producing magnesium components for automotive, consumer electronics, and medical devices. Operates 23 manufacturing facilities across 16 countries.

companies.name: Georg Fischer Automotive
companies.use: Major European supplier of magnesium and aluminum structural castings for automotive OEMs. Produces magnesium steering column supports, crosscar beams, and door structures.

companies.name: Chicago White Metal
companies.use: Specialty die-caster producing magnesium and aluminum components for automotive, defense, and industrial applications. Known for large, thin-wall magnesium structural castings.

industries.name: Steel Desulfurization
uses: Injection of granular magnesium metal into molten iron in ladle desulfurization stations — magnesium reacts with sulfur to form MgS slag, reducing sulfur below 0.003%
Co-injection of magnesium with lime (calcium oxide) to optimize reaction kinetics and sulfide flotation in hot metal desulfurization
Low-sulfur steel required for automotive exposed panels, electrical steel, and high-strength structural steel — sulfur causes red-shortness and impairs surface quality
Magnesium desulfurization is faster and more efficient than older CaC₂ (calcium carbide) injection methods at comparable cost
Nodular (ductile) cast iron production requires magnesium addition to molten iron to convert graphite flakes to spheroidal nodules — dramatically improving toughness and ductility

companies.name: ArcelorMittal
companies.use: World's largest steel company; consumes significant magnesium metal for hot metal desulfurization at integrated blast furnace steelworks across Europe, North America, and Asia.

companies.name: Nippon Steel
companies.use: Japan's largest steelmaker; operates magnesium desulfurization at all integrated steelworks. Produces ultra-low sulfur steel for automotive exposed surfaces and electrical applications.

companies.name: US Steel
companies.use: Major integrated steelmaker using magnesium injection for hot metal desulfurization at Gary Works and Mon Valley Works to produce low-sulfur flat-rolled steel for automotive and appliance markets.

companies.name: Hickman, Williams & Company
companies.use: Leading supplier of magnesium granules and co-injection reagents for steel desulfurization. Also supplies magnesium for ductile iron production to North American foundries.

industries.name: Aluminum Alloy Production
uses: Magnesium is the primary alloying element in 5xxx series aluminum alloys (Al-Mg) used for marine, transportation, and structural applications — 0.5–5% Mg addition dramatically increases strength

6xxx series aluminum alloys (Al-Mg-Si) — the most widely used structural aluminum — require magnesium for precipitation hardening response; used in automotive extrusions, aerospace structures, and bicycle frames
7xxx series (Al-Zn-Mg-Cu) high-strength aerospace alloys (7075, 7050) use magnesium to maximize strength through age hardening — used in aircraft wing spars and fuselage frames
Magnesium grain-refines aluminum castings, reducing hot tearing and improving surface quality in die-cast and sand-cast products
Beverage can body stock (3004 alloy: Al-Mn-Mg) uses ~1% magnesium for work hardening and dent resistance

companies.name: Norsk Hydro
companies.use: Fully integrated aluminum producer from bauxite to finished product. Major consumer of magnesium metal for alloying 5xxx, 6xxx, and 7xxx series alloys for automotive, aerospace, and building applications.

companies.name: Novelis (Hindalco)
companies.use: World's largest aluminum rolling company. Produces 5xxx series automotive body sheet and 3xxx series can body stock, both requiring magnesium additions for strength and formability.

companies.name: Constellium
companies.use: Aerospace and automotive aluminum specialist. Major producer of 7xxx and 6xxx series alloys requiring precise magnesium additions for Airbus, Boeing, and automotive structural applications.

industries.name: Refractory Materials & Chemicals
uses: Dead-burned magnesia (MgO, periclase) produced by high-temperature calcination of magnesite ore — principal refractory for steel BOF furnace linings, cement kiln bricks, and glass tank walls
Fused magnesia (electrofused MgO) for premium refractory bricks in electric arc furnaces, ladle refractories, and high-wear steel plant applications
Caustic-calcined magnesia for magnesium oxide wallboard (fire-resistant building panels), animal feed supplements, and wastewater treatment (neutralizing acids)
Magnesium hydroxide Mg(OH)₂ as flame retardant filler in polyolefin cables and polymers — releases water vapor when heated, cooling and diluting combustible gases
Magnesium chloride (MgCl₂) for dust suppression on unpaved roads, concrete acceleration, and production of magnesium metal via electrolysis

companies.name: RHI Magnesita
companies.use: World's largest refractory producer. Mines magnesite and produces dead-burned and fused magnesia for steel, cement, glass, and non-ferrous metallurgy furnace linings globally.

companies.name: Grecian Magnesite
companies.use: Major European magnesite miner and magnesia producer. Supplies dead-burned, caustic-calcined, and fused magnesia for refractory and industrial applications worldwide.

companies.name: Martin Marietta Magnesia Specialties
companies.use: Leading North American producer of magnesium hydroxide and magnesium oxide for environmental, industrial, and agricultural applications including wastewater treatment and animal feed.
